metric: Club soccer annual participation cost vs school soccer
sample_size: Four-figure club vs modest school fees
column12: Club soccer costs described as "four-figure sums now considered normal"; school fees "modest" by comparison; article argues schools underutilized as soccer development platform

metric: School soccer structural advantage
sample_size: Multi-year coach observation vs 1-2 showcase weekends
column12: School coaches can observe players over full academic career; college scouts at school games watch players over time vs club showcase weekends; recruiting window advantage for school-based exposure

metric: Club vs school funding model
column12: School soccer funded via public budgets and communities vs club model where families bear full cost; policy tension: school system underused as soccer development base due to dominance of pay-to-play club model

metric: Share of college soccer players who reach MLS or NWSL
notes: Less than 2% of college soccer players move into MLS/NWSL; increasing share of pros now come through MLS NEXT Pro and USL academies rather than college

metric: Total MLS NEXT clubs players teams (2025-26)
column12: Doubled previous player and club pool; TWO tiers: Homegrown Division (MLS academies + elite non-MLS) and Academy Division (regionalized broader clubs); scholarship initiative: 1 fully-funded spot per club per season required

metric: Total AYSO players and teams
column12: 600k+ players; 50k teams; Everyone Plays guarantee; volunteer coaches; no tryouts; most affordable entry point; notable alumni: Landon Donovan Alex Morgan

metric: College soccer to pro conversion rate by division
notes: Less than 2% of any college level reach major pro leagues (MLS/NWSL); increasing pro pipeline through academies/USL rather than college route

metric: Fee parity across flight tiers within large clubs
sample_description: Large multi-team clubs (Del Mar Surf model)
notes: QUALITATIVE: lower-flight families pay SAME annual fee as top-flight families but receive worse practice times, lower-quality coaches, and fill-in coaches from higher-priority teams; equal price unequal product is core business model of large franchise clubs

metric: Private equity ownership of youth soccer club brands
sample_size: Del Mar Surf / Surf Soccer national brand
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: Surf Soccer described as national for-profit corporation that licenses brand to local clubs; partially owned by private equity fund; revenue model: maximize team count to sell more kits, collect more tournament fees, registration fees; brand prestige used to justify premium pricing regardless of coaching quality

metric: Coach quality concentration at large multi-team clubs
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: best coaches assigned to Flight 1 teams; coaches below top team may manage 4+ teams simultaneously and send fill-ins to non-priority games; top Flight 1 teams succeed in spite of club coaching because elite players do private training and futsal outside of club; coach quality is the key variable not club brand

metric: Political dynamics in club soccer tryouts
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: new/unknown players grouped separately at tryouts with minimal coach attention; placement often based on prior relationship not observed ability; top teams frequently full before tryouts open; unknown players underplaced in year 1 regardless of talent; rec all-star status (e.g. RB/PQ rec) helps flag talent to SDSC coaches

metric: Player-to-roster ratio impact on development
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: large clubs carry 18 players per team = fewer minutes per player; smaller clubs carry 10-12 players = significantly more game time per player; more minutes = better development especially at younger ages (U10-U13); smaller cheaper clubs may produce better individual development than expensive brand-name clubs

metric: Annual fee structure at large SoCal club (Del Mar Surf)
sample_size: Del Mar Surf SoCal U10 level
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: no specific dollar figure confirmed in thread; described as costing more than SDSC or Force competitors; families on lower-flight teams pay premium rates for reduced coaching access and inferior practice slot; cost-to-value ratio deteriorates significantly below Flight 1

metric: Coach quality vs club brand as development predictor
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: multiple experienced parents converge on same conclusion: individual coach quality is more predictive of player development than club brand or tier; a strong coach at a smaller/cheaper club outperforms a weak coach at a prestigious expensive club; parents advised to identify coaches before choosing clubs

metric: Field cancellations as hidden cost at premium clubs
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: Del Mar Surf fields close when it rains; more out-of-SD tournament travel than comparable clubs; described as 'more cost for less soccer' vs cheaper competitors; hidden cost of premium clubs = reduced actual training time even at same annual fee

metric: Supplemental training cost differential between large and small clubs
sample_size: SDSC Surf vs Del Mar Surf
ref_id: QUALITATIVE: Del Mar Surf charges extra for additional clinics and training beyond base fee; SDSC Surf includes additional clinic trainings at NO extra charge at same or lower base price; hidden cost differential between large brand club and smaller club can be significant across a season

metric: Travel time as hidden cost of youth soccer
notes: QUALITATIVE: 45-60 min drive each way for practice is common in San Diego county; parents drive to drop off then return home then drive back; real time cost ~3-4 hrs per practice session; North County traffic described as brutal; commute time and gas are uncounted costs in most club fee analyses

metric: MLS NEXT clubs in San Diego county by geography
notes: 4 MLS NEXT clubs in San Diego intentionally distributed geographically: Chula Vista FC (south) / Albion (central Ocean Beach+Kearny Mesa) / City SC (north Carlsbad) / San Diego FC Academy (east - brand new); geographic distribution reduces cross-town travel for players

metric: SoCal club quality-to-cost tiers (community assessment)
ref_id: QUALITATIVE ranking by experienced parents: Del Mar Surf=highest cost/top talent/inconsistent development/drama; Albion=high touches/physical style/MLS NEXT; SDSC Surf=grassroots/lower cost/additional training included; City SC=good value/strong coaches at all levels/MLS NEXT; Legends=not where elite players end up; strategic advice: choose club where child makes A team rather than riding bench at prestige club
